homeFaq.headline: Got
homeFaq.subtext: Everything you need to know about finding local farms and using Farmsy.
homeFaq.a1: Browsing the full farm map requires a Farmsy Premium subscription — €29.99/year or €49.99 for lifetime access. Every new account starts with a 3-day free trial, no charge until day 3. Cancel before then and you won't be billed.
homeFaq.a2: We currently cover the Netherlands and Belgium. Farm listings come from OpenStreetMap, Foursquare, and Overture Maps. We are working to improve coverage in both countries.
homeFaq.q3: Where does the farm data come from?
homeFaq.a3: Our listings are built from a mix of four sources: OpenStreetMap, Foursquare, Overture Maps, and Traces. Phone numbers, websites, photos, and location data are combined from all four to give you the most complete picture possible.
homeFaq.q4: How do I claim my farm listing?
homeFaq.a4: Find your farm on the map and open its detail panel. Tap "Claim this farm" and sign in with your email. Once verified, you can update your contact details, opening hours, description, and more. Claiming is free.
homeFaq.q5: How often is the data updated?
homeFaq.a5: Data is regularly reviewed by our team. The best way to keep a listing current is for the farm owner to claim it — claimed listings can be edited directly and changes appear immediately.
homeFaq.q6: Do you have a mobile app?
homeFaq.a6: Not yet — but our website is fully responsive and works great on mobile. You can add it to your home screen for an app-like experience. A native app is on our roadmap.

cta.subtext: Join the pre-launch list. No spam — just one email when Farmsy goes live.

about.headline: Connecting people with the
about.subheading: Farmsy is a free map of local farms, roadside stands, and direct-to-consumer food producers in the Netherlands and Belgium.
about.mission1: The modern food system has created a gap between our plates and the people who grow our food. At Farmsy, we believe that transparency is the first step toward a more sustainable future. Our mission is to bridge this gap by making local food discoverable for everyone. We map every farm shop, pick-your-own field, roadside egg stand, and artisan producer we can find — and make that information freely available to anyone seeking a deeper connection to their food source.
about.mission2: We focus our efforts on the Netherlands and Belgium, regions with an incredible heritage of small-scale agriculture and direct-to-consumer sales. By consolidating data from multiple verified sources, we provide a comprehensive directory that celebrates the diversity of local producers, from large-scale organic dairies to small family-run orchards.
about.mission3: We are committed to supporting the farming community directly. For producers, listing and claiming your business on Farmsy is entirely free. This allows you to manage your own story — updating your seasonal products, opening hours, and contact details — ensuring that when consumers find you, they have the most accurate and up-to-date information possible.
about.v1Desc: We believe fresh food tastes better and is better for the planet when it travels fewer kilometres from field to fork.
about.v2Desc: Our core dataset is built on OpenStreetMap — a community-driven, open geographic database anyone can contribute to.
about.v3Desc: Farmers can claim their listing for free and keep their own information accurate — hours, contact details, and more.
about.v4Desc: We clearly attribute every data source — OpenStreetMap, Foursquare, Overture Maps, and Traces — on every listing where it applies.
about.sourcesSubheading: We rely on open and attributed data. Below is a transparent breakdown of where our farm information comes from.
about.osmDesc: The open geographic database that powers our core farm locations, addresses, and opening hours. Data contributed by thousands of volunteers worldwide.
about.foursquareDesc: Used to enrich listings with additional business details such as phone numbers, websites, and photos.
about.overtureDesc: Open map data from the Overture Maps Foundation, used to fill gaps in coverage and add verified location data.
about.tracesDesc: Location intelligence platform providing supplementary contact information and business details for farm listings.
about.osmNotice: Farm listings from OpenStreetMap are © OpenStreetMap contributors, available under the <link>Open Database License</link>.
about.nlDesc: Full coverage via OpenStreetMap. Includes farm shops, pick-your-own, roadside stands, honey producers, dairies, markets, and more.
about.beDesc: Coverage via OpenStreetMap, Foursquare, Overture Maps, and Traces — covering Flanders, Wallonia, and Brussels.

faq.headline: Frequently asked
faq.subheading: Everything you need to know about finding local farms and using Farmsy.
faq.q1: How do I find farms near me?
faq.a1: Open the <mapLink>interactive map</mapLink> and use the "Locate me" button (crosshair icon) to jump to your location. You can also type a city or postal code. Tap any pin to see farm details.
faq.a2: Browsing the full farm map requires a Farmsy Premium subscription — €29.99/year or €49.99 lifetime (one-time, no renewals). Every new account gets a 3-day free trial — your card is collected upfront but not charged until day 3. Cancel before then and you won't be billed. Claiming your farm listing is always free.
faq.q3: How do I claim my farm listing?
faq.a3: Find your farm on the map and open its detail panel. Tap "Claim this farm" and sign in with your email. Once verified, you can update your contact details, opening hours, description, and more. Claiming is free.
faq.a4: We currently cover the Netherlands and Belgium. Listings come from OpenStreetMap, Foursquare, Overture Maps, and Traces. We are continuously improving coverage in both countries.
faq.q5: Where does the farm data come from?
faq.a5: Our data is built from four sources: OpenStreetMap, Foursquare, Overture Maps, and Traces. Phone numbers, websites, photos, and locations are combined from all four for the most complete picture possible.
faq.q6: How often is the data updated?
faq.a6: Data is regularly reviewed by our team. The best way to keep a listing current is for the farm owner to claim it — claimed listings can be edited directly and changes appear immediately. If you spot something outdated, <contactLink>let us know</contactLink>.
faq.q7: Can I suggest a farm to add?
faq.a7: Yes — <contactLink>contact us</contactLink> with the farm's name and location and we'll review it. You can also add it to <osmLink>OpenStreetMap</osmLink> — open geographic data we draw from when building our database.
faq.q8: Why is my farm information wrong?
faq.a8: If you own the farm, the fastest fix is to claim your listing — you can then edit everything directly. If you're a visitor who spotted an error, use the "Report incorrect info" link at the bottom of the farm detail panel, or contact us.
faq.a9: We collect only what we need. Visitors have no personal data collected beyond anonymous analytics. Account holders have their email stored securely via Supabase Auth. See our <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink> for full details.
faq.q10: Do you have a mobile app?
faq.a10: Not yet — but our website is fully responsive and works great on mobile. You can add it to your home screen for an app-like experience. A native app is on our roadmap.
faq.ctaSubtext: We're happy to help. Send us a message and we'll get back to you within one business day.

contact.headline: We'd love to
contact.subheading: Whether you have questions about finding farms, need help with your subscription, want to report incorrect farm data, or just want to say hello — we're here. We typically respond within one business day.
contact.disclaimer: This opens your email app. No data is sent to our servers from this form.
contact.successTitle: Message opened in your email app
contact.successMessage: Your email client should have opened with the message pre-filled. Send it from there and we'll get back to you soon.
contact.farmOwnerDesc: Questions about claiming your listing, updating your information, or removing your farm.
contact.supportDesc: Can't find a farm, spotted an error, or need help? We'll get back to you quickly.
contact.feedbackDesc: Ideas for new features, farms we should add, or anything you'd like to see improve.

farmers.headline: Get found by
farmers.subheading: Farmsy helps consumers across the Netherlands and Belgium discover farms near them. Claim your listing to keep your information accurate and attract more visitors.
farmers.b1Desc: Your farm is shown to consumers searching nearby for fresh, local produce.
farmers.b2Desc: Update your name, address, opening hours, and contact details at any time.
farmers.b3Desc: Connect directly with people who want to buy local — no middlemen.
farmers.b4Desc: Claiming and managing your farm listing costs nothing. No subscription required for farm owners.
farmers.step1: Search for your farm on the map — it may already be listed from public data sources.
farmers.step2: Create a free account using your email address.
farmers.step3: Claim your listing and verify you are the owner or operator.
farmers.step4: Update your details: hours, products, photos, and contact info.
